title: Handle Tableau Error with Redirect When PAT Is Missing or Expired
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to be redirected to the settings page if Tableau is not set up, and see a clear error message if my Personal Access Token has expired, instead of seeing a broken page.
description:
Error handling improvement for Tableau integration:

1. **No PAT configured**: If the user has not added a Tableau Personal Access Token, redirect them automatically to the settings page where they can add one
2. **Expired/invalid PAT**: If a PAT exists but is expired or invalid (Tableau server removed it due to system PAT policy), show a clear error message explaining the issue
3. **Spelling fix**: 'Tableau' was misspelled in the UI — corrected
acceptance_criteria: Users without a PAT are redirected to settings page
Users with an expired PAT see a clear error message
Error message explains the likely cause (PAT expiry)
ux_changes: Added redirect to settings when no PAT exists; clear error message for expired PATs; fixed 'Tableau' spelling.
middleware_changes: Added PAT existence check and validation logic to handle expired/invalid tokens gracefully.
implementation_notes: PAT expiration is driven by Tableau Server's system policy. The app now checks PAT status before making Tableau API calls and routes users accordingly.
comment_summary: Waqar implemented conditional redirect: no PAT redirects to settings, expired PAT shows an error message with guidance.
